title: North Korea-Russia announce five-year military cooperation plan for 2027-2031
summary: Russian Defense Minister Belousov proposed a formal 2027-2031 military cooperation plan with North Korea during Pyongyang talks, signaling a shift from transactional wartime partnership to institutionalized alliance with technology transfers and joint training.
contents:
## North Korea-Russia Five-Year Military Plan

### Formal Agreement Proposed
Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belousov told North Korean leader Kim Jong Un during talks in Pyongyang that Moscow stands prepared to sign a "Russian-Korean military cooperation plan for the 2027-2031 period" this year. Belousov stated they reached agreement to place military cooperation "on a sustainable, long-term footing."

### Scope of Cooperation
Analysts assess the plan could include technology transfers and joint production, regularized military exercises, personnel exchanges, and supply chain integration. Professor Lim Eul-chul of Kyungnam University described the development as a shift beyond a "transactional partnership based on necessity" into a "long-term blood alliance."

### Memorial Ceremony
Belousov and Russian State Duma Speaker Volodin visited Pyongyang to attend the opening of a memorial honoring North Korean troops killed while fighting alongside Russia in Ukraine. Kim described the memorial as symbolizing ties "forged in blood" and pledged continued support for Russia's sovereignty defense.

### Security Implications
Over five years, the plan could enable North Korea to significantly modernize conventional weapons while advancing satellite, nuclear-powered submarine, and next-generation missile technologies. Since 2024, North Korea has deployed roughly 15,000 troops to support Russia's war in Ukraine, with approximately 6,000 killed or wounded.
